data.content:
## Implementation Plan: Shared Interactive Agent Session Runtime

### Problem Statement

Terminal-manager hard-codes isInteractive = dialect === "claude" in both initial-child (line 90) and take-child (line 62) spawn paths. This means:
- Only Claude gets stdin piped; all others get stdin: "ignore"
- Follow-up prompts, auto-answer, and input close timing are Claude-only
- IO wiring code is duplicated across initial-child and take-child with subtle differences
- Adding Gemini (detected in agent-identity but missing from AgentDialect) or any new interactive agent requires touching multiple files
- There is no way for an agent to declare its capabilities

### Design: AgentSessionCapabilities Interface

Create a new file agent-session-capabilities.ts with a capability descriptor per dialect:

interface AgentSessionCapabilities {
  interactive: boolean;
  promptTransport: "stdin" | "cli-arg";
  followUpSupport: boolean;
  turnResultEvent: string;
  sessionEndDetection: "process-exit" | "stream-signal";
  drainStdinOnResult: boolean;
  inputCloseGraceMs: number;
  inactivityTimeoutMs: number;
  autoAnswerAskUser: boolean;
}

Capability matrix (Claude=interactive+stdin+followUp+drain+autoAnswer, Codex/Copilot/OpenCode/Gemini=one-shot+cli-arg, Copilot also gets autoAnswer).

### Implementation Steps

Step 1: Add Gemini to AgentDialect and create capability registry
Files: agent-adapter.ts, new agent-session-capabilities.ts
- Add gemini to AgentDialect union
- Add Gemini detection in resolveDialect()
- Create DIALECT_CAPABILITIES map with getSessionCapabilities() export

Step 2: Add Gemini normalizer and arg builder
Files: agent-adapter.ts
- Add Gemini branch in buildPromptModeArgs() (one-shot like Codex)
- Add identity normalizer for Gemini in createLineNormalizer()

Step 3: Extract shared session IO runtime
Files: new agent-session-io.ts
- Extract duplicated IO patterns from initial-io and take-child into shared module
- createChildIoState(caps), closeInput(), scheduleInputClose(), sendUserTurn(), autoAnswerAskUser(), handleResultEvent()
- Both initial-io and take-child import from shared module

Step 4: Replace hard-coded isInteractive checks
Files: terminal-manager-initial-child.ts, terminal-manager-take-child.ts
- Replace dialect === "claude" with caps.interactive from getSessionCapabilities()
- Pass caps to shared IO functions instead of raw booleans

Step 5: Centralize process-group kill
Files: terminal-manager.ts
- Extract killProcessGroup() helper (already generic, minor cleanup)

Step 6: Verify post-exit hooks are dialect-agnostic
Files: terminal-manager-take-iteration.ts
- Confirm handleTakeIterationClose chain is not dialect-gated (it already works generically)

Step 7: Conformance tests
Files: new agent-session-capabilities.test.ts, new agent-session-io.test.ts
- Capability registry: every dialect has valid entry
- Turn completion: result event triggers scheduleInputClose (interactive) or no-op
- Session exit: process close triggers cleanup for all dialects
- Timeout handling: grace period respects caps.inputCloseGraceMs
- Auto-answer: gated by caps.autoAnswerAskUser
- Abort: process-group kill works uniformly

### Complexity: Medium (4-6 focused sessions)
Steps 1-2: Low | Step 3: Medium (careful refactoring) | Step 4: Low | Steps 5-6: Low | Step 7: Medium

### Risks
1. Subtle IO divergence between initial-child and take-child (auto-ship follow-up only in initial). Must preserve via composition.
2. Gemini JSON format unknown — use identity normalizer + TODO.
3. Child process mocking needs care for test isolation.
